issueTitle: The Gut, the Heart, and the Aging Brain
editorialThesis: This week's selected evidence spans microbial stress, cardiovascular treatment choice, movement, weight management, and multidomain cognitive prevention; the signals are worthwhile, but they do not establish one shared mechanism.

sources.title: Lactiplantibacillus plantarum (WJL) ameliorates chronic kidney disease by inhibiting fibroblast growth factor 21 adaptive stress response via low protein diet.
sources.sourcePhrase: A preclinical microbial intervention study reports a kidney-stress response in the context of a low-protein diet.

sources.title: Impact of carvedilol versus metoprolol on ventricular arrhythmia burden and ICD therapies: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
sources.sourcePhrase: A systematic review compares two beta blockers for ventricular arrhythmia burden and ICD therapy outcomes.

sources.title: Physical activity for the management of obesity in children up to the age of 9 years.
sources.sourcePhrase: A Cochrane review evaluates physical activity for obesity management in children aged nine years or younger.

sources.title: Efficacy and tolerability of nutraceuticals and phytotherapics for antipsychotic-induced weight gain in schizophrenia spectrum disorders: Systematic review and meta-analysis.
sources.sourcePhrase: A systematic review examines nutraceutical and phytotherapy approaches to antipsychotic-associated weight gain.

sources.title: Multidomain lifestyle intervention for the prevention of cognitive decline in at-risk older adults in Latin America (LatAm-FINGERS): a single-blind, multicentre, randomised controlled trial.
sources.sourcePhrase: A multicentre randomized trial studies a multidomain lifestyle intervention for cognitive decline prevention in at-risk older adults in Latin America.

direction.centralTension: Five useful observations share one week of attention without resolving into one biological explanation.
direction.coverPaperRole: The multidomain cognitive-prevention trial is an entry point into the week's questions, not a claim of greater importance or a mechanism linking all five papers.
direction.physicalWorld: A shallow, quiet tabletop world of weathered natural and domestic forms arranged on warm paper in cool morning light.
direction.heroDescription: Five distinct forms sit with generous intervals and individual shadows; none touches another, and no form dominates the group.
direction.controlledImpossibility: Only the seed pod's shadow stops a finger-width before the object that should cast it.
direction.unresolvedState: The arrangement feels poised for comparison, but its five parts remain deliberately separate.
direction.supportedInterpretation: The week contains several credible lines of inquiry across different interventions, populations, and outcomes.
direction.principalUncertainty: The studies differ in population, intervention, endpoint, and evidentiary context, so their coexistence does not establish a shared gut-heart-brain pathway.
claimsNotImply: That the five studies demonstrate a shared biological mechanism.
That association or intervention evidence in one population establishes causation in another.
That one medicine, supplement, lifestyle program, or dietary approach is clinically recommended.
That findings generalize across children, adults with schizophrenia, kidney-disease models, cardiac populations, and at-risk older adults.
That the visually featured paper is the week's most important evidence.
